strategy_research: {"strategies": [{"name": "Vigilant Asset Allocation (VAA) \u2014 Breadth Momentum", "description": "Monthly rotation using a weighted momentum score (12/4/2/1 weighting across 1/3/6/12-month returns). If ANY offensive asset scores negative, 100% rotates to the highest-scoring defensive asset. Breadth-based crash protection is the core innovation.", "evidence": ["Keller & Keuning (2017) 'Breadth Momentum and Vigilant Asset Allocation' \u2014 SSRN abstract_id=3002624", "Keller & Keuning (2018) 'Breadth Momentum and the Canary Universe: Defensive Asset Allocation (DAA)' \u2014 SSRN", "AllocateSmartly independent replication and live tracking (allocatesmartly.com)"], "historical_sharpe": "0.94\u20131.10 (in-sample 1970\u20132016); G4 Aggressive variant ~1.10 with AGG in universe", "market_conditions": "Works: sustained bear markets with gradual breadth deterioration (2001\u20132002, 2008\u20132009, 2022). Fails: V-shaped crashes (COVID March 2020) where recovery is missed; concentrated bull markets (2023\u20132024 Mag-7) where a single false defensive signal is costly; high-whipsaw environments near the breadth threshold.", "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "IWM", "EFA", "GLD", "TLT", "AGG"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "medium"}, {"name": "Antonacci's Dual Momentum (GEM)", "description": "Three-asset monthly system: first check if SPY beats T-Bills over 12 months (absolute momentum) \u2014 if not, hold AGG. If yes, compare SPY vs. EFA 12-month return (relative momentum) and hold the winner. Harvests both absolute and relative momentum risk premia.", "evidence": ["Antonacci (2012/2016) 'Risk Premia Harvesting Through Dual Momentum' \u2014 SSRN", "Antonacci (2014) 'Dual Momentum Investing' \u2014 McGraw-Hill", "Robot Wealth independent out-of-sample review (robotwealth.com/dual-momentum-review)", "AllocateSmartly live tracking and replication"], "historical_sharpe": "0.87 in-sample (1974\u20132013) vs. 0.22 for S&P 500 same period; 0.80\u20131.00 over full cycles; post-publication 2015\u20132019 underperformed benchmark slightly", "market_conditions": "Works: sustained bear markets where 12-month momentum holds direction for months (2000\u20132002, 2008). Fails: V-shaped recoveries (COVID 2020) where 12-month lookback is too slow; prolonged US outperformance vs. international (2015\u20132024) causing false EFA signals; 2022 where both SPY and AGG fell simultaneously.", "instruments": ["SPY", "EFA", "AGG"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "low"}, {"name": "Mean Reversion with VIX Spike Filter", "description": "Buy SPY when it closes below its 20/30-day moving average AND VIX exceeds a threshold (25\u201335). Hold TLT or GLD during elevated-VIX periods as a defensive position. Exit when SPY recrosses its moving average or hits a stop-loss. Exploits the empirically robust mean-reverting nature of VIX and the negative VIX-equity correlation.", "evidence": ["Nielsen & Posselt (2024) 'Betting on Mean Reversion in the VIX' \u2014 ScienceDirect / SSRN abstract_id=3529285", "Barroso & Santa-Clara (2015) 'Momentum Has Its Moments' \u2014 demonstrated volatility-scaling doubles Sharpe", "Cboe empirical literature on VIX mean-reversion as stylized fact", "Price Action Lab (2024) regime-switching SPY/QQQ/TLT/GLD backtest \u2014 Sharpe exceeding 1.15"], "historical_sharpe": "0.77\u20131.15 depending on VIX threshold and exit rules; backtested SPY variant with Bollinger Band exits showed 75.3% win rate (2006\u20132018)", "market_conditions": "Works: short, sharp fear-driven selloffs with quick recovery (2011, 2015, 2018 Q4, early 2020). Fails: sustained bear markets where elevated VIX is not transient but signals multi-month decline (2008, 2022 early stages); simultaneous stock-bond decline (2022 inflation regime).", "instruments": ["SPY", "TLT", "GLD"], "timeframe": "daily", "complexity": "low"}, {"name": "Cross-Asset Momentum Ranking + 10-Month Trend Filter", "description": "Rank all 8 ETFs monthly by 1/3/12-month composite return. Hold the top 1\u20133 ranked assets, but only if they are above their 10-month (200-day) SMA. Rotate excluded allocations to AGG. Combines relative momentum (cross-sectional) with absolute momentum (trend filter) for a dual layer of crash protection.", "evidence": ["Moskowitz, Ooi & Pedersen (2011) 'Time Series Momentum' \u2014 Journal of Financial Economics", "Hurst, Ooi & Pedersen (2012) 'Two Centuries of Multi-Asset Momentum'", "CME/JPMorgan (2015) 'Momentum Strategies Across Asset Classes' \u2014 EMV portfolio Sharpe 0.78 (1972\u20132014)", "EFMA (2013) 'Risk Parity, Momentum and Trend Following in Global Asset Allocation' \u2014 trend filter adds 0.3\u20130.5 Sharpe", "Hanauer & Windm\u00fcller (2019) enhanced momentum \u2014 Sharpe and t-stats roughly doubled vs. standard momentum"], "historical_sharpe": "0.78 base (EMV-weighted, 1972\u20132014); +0.30\u20130.50 improvement with trend filter; max drawdowns under 10% for top-half momentum portfolio with filter", "market_conditions": "Works: trending markets with high cross-asset dispersion (2001\u20132002, 2008\u20132009, 2020\u20132021, 2022 for bonds/gold). Fails: low-dispersion mean-reverting markets; momentum crashes after prolonged momentum-chasing; late bull markets with frequent sector rotation causing rank instability; COVID March 2020 rapid reversal.", "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "IWM", "VTI", "GLD", "TLT", "AGG", "EFA"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "low"}, {"name": "Regime-Based Rotation: VIX + Yield Curve Signals", "description": "Define two regimes using VIX level and 10Y-2Y yield curve spread. Low-vol/expansion (VIX < 20 AND curve positive): hold SPY, QQQ, IWM. High-vol/contraction (VIX > 25 OR curve inverted): rotate to TLT, GLD, AGG. A simple threshold version captures most of the benefit of the ML-based HMM academic versions without requiring statistical modeling.", "evidence": ["arXiv (Oct 2024) 'Dynamic Factor Allocation Leveraging Regime-Switching Signals' \u2014 regime-switching outperforms static on Sharpe and IR", "Nature Scientific Reports (2025) LSTM + regime + VIX + yield curve \u2014 Sharpe 1.38 OOS (2017\u20132022), +55% vs. risk parity", "MDPI Journal of Risk and Financial Management (2020) 'Regime-Switching Factor Investing with Hidden Markov Models'", "Ang & Bekaert (2004) foundational empirical paper on regime-based asset allocation OOS benefit", "Splendor (2026) HMM study: low-vol regime SPY Sharpe 2.66 vs. high-vol regime TLT Sharpe 1.27"], "historical_sharpe": "0.90\u20131.10 for simple threshold version; 1.38 OOS for ML-enhanced LSTM version (2017\u20132022); high-VIX regime rotation showed +187% higher Sharpe vs. traditional risk parity", "market_conditions": "Works: gradual regime transitions (2001\u20132002, 2008 onset); persistent volatility clustering; clear yield curve signals. Fails: rapid regime transitions (COVID 2020); ambiguous regimes (VIX at 24, curve marginally positive); 2022 where yield curve inverted late after equities already corrected 20%.", "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "IWM", "TLT", "GLD", "AGG", "EFA"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "medium"}, {"name": "Hierarchical Risk Parity (HRP)", "description": "L\u00f3pez de Prado's 2016 method: cluster assets by correlation similarity via hierarchical dendrogram, quasi-diagonalize the covariance matrix, then allocate capital inversely proportional to variance at each dendrogram branch via recursive bisection. Eliminates matrix inversion and produces more stable out-of-sample weights than Markowitz MVO.", "evidence": ["L\u00f3pez de Prado (2016) 'Building Diversified Portfolios that Outperform Out-of-Sample' \u2014 Journal of Portfolio Management, SSRN abstract_id=2708678", "L\u00f3pez de Prado (2018) 'Advances in Financial Machine Learning' \u2014 Wiley", "ETF empirical study: mean Sharpe 0.47 with high rolling std dev; SPY-TLT-GLD risk parity variant Sharpe ~1.28", "OOS Monte Carlo (original paper): HRP improves Sharpe by 31.3% over CLA; OOS variance 72.47% lower than Markowitz"], "historical_sharpe": "0.47\u20131.28 (wide range depending on universe and period); OOS variance 72% lower than Markowitz MVO; 2008 ETF backtest showed -60% max drawdown \u2014 does not protect against systemic crises", "market_conditions": "Works: stable correlation regimes; environments where risk-budgeting matters more than market timing; normal risk-off/risk-on with diversified asset classes. Fails: correlation breakdown (2022 stocks and bonds fell together); systemic crises erasing diversification assumptions; short-term \u2014 HRP is not a tactical strategy and does not avoid bear markets.", "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "IWM", "VTI", "GLD", "TLT", "AGG", "EFA"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "high"}, {"name": "VIX Term Structure Signal (Contango/Backwardation Filter)", "description": "Use the VIX term structure as a market regime filter: when VIX < VIX3M (contango, >80% of time since 2010), overweight equities (SPY, QQQ); when VIX > VIX3M (backwardation, fear spike), rotate to TLT and GLD as a contrarian signal. Used as a filter layer on top of a base rotation strategy rather than direct VIX futures trading.", "evidence": ["QuantPedia 'Exploiting Term Structure of VIX Futures' \u2014 empirical strategy database entry", "Macrosynergy Research 'VIX Term Structure as a Trading Signal'", "PMC/NIH (2024) 'VIX Constant Maturity Futures Trading Strategy: Walk-Forward ML Study' \u2014 Random Forest Sharpe 2.24\u20132.60, >94.8% accuracy in predicting contango state", "VRP term structure strategy: Sharpe 0.77 vs. 0.01 naive baseline (2006\u20132016)", "Logical Invest Maximum Yield ETF Rotation using VIX contango: ~Sharpe 1.32"], "historical_sharpe": "0.77\u20131.32 as a filter signal; 2.24\u20132.60 for ML-based direct VIX futures trading (likely partially overfitted); wide range reflects implementation method", "market_conditions": "Works: calm, range-bound markets with persistent contango (2012\u20132019); short-lived panic spikes. Fails: sustained volatility regimes with months-long backwardation (2008, 2022); extreme tail events like February 2018 Volmageddon; direct VIX short exposure is catastrophic in blow-up scenarios.", "instruments": ["SPY", "QQQ", "TLT", "GLD", "AGG"], "timeframe": "daily", "complexity": "medium"}, {"name": "Intermarket Divergence \u2014 SPY/TLT/GLD Universal Investment Strategy", "description": "Monthly Sharpe-maximizing rotation across SPY, TLT, and GLD using a 72-day lookback. Test all allocation combinations and select the mix maximizing a modified Sharpe (return / std_dev^2.5). Exploits the empirical 20-day lead-lag of bonds over equities and four macro regimes defined by GLD vs. TLT relative momentum (risk-on, stagflation, deflation, inflation).", "evidence": ["Logical Invest 'Universal Investment Strategy' \u2014 20-year backtest (VFINX/VUSTX proxies) Sharpe 1.28 vs. 0.50 S&P 500", "John Murphy 'Trading with Intermarket Analysis' \u2014 foundational reference", "Federal Reserve research on stock-bond correlation regime flips during stress", "Emini-Watch intermarket analysis: bond market empirically leads equities by ~20 days", "AllocateSmartly SPY-TLT rotation independent backtest and tracking"], "historical_sharpe": "1.28 for SPY-TLT blend (20-year backtest) vs. 0.50 for SPY alone and 0.79 for bonds alone; three-asset SPY+TLT+GLD version historically superior; 2022 expanded max drawdown to ~41% as stock-bond correlation turned positive", "market_conditions": "Works: traditional risk-off/risk-on regimes with negative stock-bond correlation (2000\u20132020); inflationary environments where gold leads equity deterioration; deflationary crashes where bonds rally as equities fall. Fails: 2022-style stagflation where stocks and bonds fall simultaneously; gold-bond divergence from idiosyncratic causes (dollar strength, supply shocks) rather than macro regime.", "instruments": ["SPY", "TLT", "GLD", "EFA", "AGG"], "timeframe": "monthly", "complexity": "low"}], "recommendation": "Start with the Mean Reversion + VIX Filter strategy (Strategy 3) given the stated preference for 'mean_reversion_vix_filter'. This strategy directly matches the preference family, requires only SPY, TLT, and GLD, operates on daily signals (maximizing responsiveness), has strong empirical support (Nielsen & Posselt 2024, Barroso & Santa-Clara 2015), and is executable with $1,000 capital without position-size constraints. Use a VIX threshold of 25\u201330: when SPY closes below its 30-day SMA AND VIX > 25, buy SPY; hold TLT or GLD otherwise. Layer in the VIX Term Structure Signal (Strategy 7) as a free filter \u2014 check VIX vs. VIX3M daily to confirm contango before taking equity exposure. As a second strategy, implement Cross-Asset Momentum Ranking + Trend Filter (Strategy 4) in parallel on a monthly basis \u2014 it uses all 8 instruments in your universe, has the deepest academic support (Moskowitz, Hurst, Pedersen), and the 10-month SMA filter provides crash protection that the VIX filter alone may miss in slow-moving bear markets. The combination of a daily VIX mean-reversion layer and a monthly cross-asset momentum layer creates a multi-frequency ensemble that is more robust than either approach alone."}
